label: Documented US Military Interventions (1898–2023)
source: Zunes & Laird, US Military Interventions, 2023; Congressional Research Service
contestNote: Count varies by definition of 'intervention'; some scholars count over 100 including covert operations

label: Deaths Attributed to Operation Condor
source: National Security Archive; UN Latin American Human Rights Commission
contestNote: Exact figures disputed; confirmed deaths from national truth commissions total ~35,000 for member states

label: Deaths in Nicaragua Contra War (1981–1990)
source: Nicaraguan government; Americas Watch (HRW); International Court of Justice
contestNote: US government disputed higher figures; ICJ found Contra actions violated international law

label: Victims Under Pinochet Dictatorship (1973–1990)
source: Valech Commission I & II (2004, 2011); Rettig Commission (1991)

label: Latin American Countries Targeted by US Covert/Military Ops
source: National Security Archive compilation; Church Committee reports 1975
contestNote: Count depends on whether economic/political pressure campaigns are included

label: Total US Security Aid to Colombia (Plan Colombia, 2000–2016)
source: US State Department; Congressional Research Service: Plan Colombia FY2000–FY2016

label: Disappeared in Argentina's Dirty War (1976–1983)
source: Mothers of Plaza de Mayo; CONADEP (Never Again) Report 1984
contestNote: Argentine government formally documented ~9,000 verified disappearances; human rights groups maintain 30,000 figure

label: Deaths in El Salvador Civil War (1979–1992)
source: UN El Salvador Truth Commission 1993; Americas Watch

label: Years of US Trade Embargo Against Cuba
source: US Office of Foreign Assets Control (OFAC); in effect since October 1960

label: SOA/WHINSEC Graduates (1946–2024)
source: US Army Western Hemisphere Institute for Security Cooperation (WHINSEC) records

label: Venezuelans Displaced (2015–2024)
contestNote: US sanctions are one contributing factor among many including pre-existing economic mismanagement
